proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association).
feature_001 has a monotone gradient with pfs_months: level 0 > level 1 > level 2 (increasing levels associated with shorter PFS).
feature_014 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_014 = 0.
feature_027 = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_027 = 0 (candidate treatment with positive effect).
Higher feature_025 is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher feature_019 (range 1.5-5.5) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
feature_023 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_023 = 0.
feature_006 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_006 = 0.
Higher feature_005 (long-tailed continuous) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_022 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_022 = 0 (rare ~5% indicator).

analyses.code: stats.linregress(df.feature_015, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Strong positive linear association: slope = +0.1765 months per unit of feature_015 (Pearson r = 0.766). Decile means rise monotonically from 1.33 (lowest decile) to 7.45 (highest).

analyses.code: scipy.stats.f_oneway and pairwise t-tests across feature_001 levels
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by feature_001 level: 0 -> 5.27 (n=17592), 1 -> 4.05 (n=24971), 2 -> 2.91 (n=7437). All pairwise differences highly significant (p < 1e-300 for each comparison). Monotone gradient confirmed.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on pfs_months by feature_014
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 5.05 when feature_014=0 (n=22519) vs 3.69 when feature_014=1 (n=27481). Difference = -1.352 months.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on pfs_months by feature_027
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 4.12 when feature_027=0 (n=39978) vs 5.09 when feature_027=1 (n=10022). Difference = +0.972 months. Strong positive direction supports treatment-candidate hypothesis.

analyses.code: stats.linregress(df.feature_025, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Negative slope = -0.0730 months per unit (range 0-24.6).

analyses.code: stats.linregress(df.feature_019, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Positive slope = +0.4654 months per unit. Across the 1.5-5.5 range that is roughly +1.9 months total.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on pfs_months by feature_023
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 4.45 when feature_023=0 vs 4.12 when feature_023=1. Difference = -0.327 months.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on pfs_months by feature_006
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 4.42 when feature_006=0 vs 4.11 when feature_006=1. Difference = -0.305 months.

analyses.code: stats.linregress(df.feature_005, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Slope = -0.0053 months per unit; very long right tail (max 777). The marginal slope is small but highly significant (p~3e-17).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on pfs_months by feature_022
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 4.32 when feature_022=0 vs 4.09 when feature_022=1 (rare, n=2272). Difference = -0.228 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015's association with pfs_months is approximately linear over the 30-90 range (no large deviations from linearity in decile means).
The negative effect of feature_014 = 1 on pfs_months is consistent across all three levels of feature_001 (no effect-modification by stage-like feature_001).
feature_027 (candidate treatment) is associated with longer pfs_months within every level of feature_001, indicating the treatment helps regardless of disease level.

analyses.code: df['_d']=pd.qcut(feature_015,10); df.groupby('_d').pfs_months.mean()
analyses.result_summary: Decile means: 1.33, 2.41, 3.09, 3.58, 4.06, 4.55, 5.00, 5.52, 6.21, 7.45 - approximately linear (constant ~+0.7 month increase per decile across the range).

analyses.code: pd.pivot_table(df, index='feature_001', columns='feature_014', values='pfs_months')
analyses.result_summary: PFS [feature_001, feature_014]: (0,0)=6.04 (0,1)=4.64; (1,0)=4.79 (1,1)=3.44; (2,0)=3.61 (2,1)=2.34. Within-stratum decrement from feature_014=1 is roughly -1.4 to -1.3 in every stratum, supporting effect-additivity.

analyses.code: pd.pivot_table(df, index='feature_001', columns='feature_027', values='pfs_months')
analyses.result_summary: PFS [feature_001, feature_027]: (0,0)=5.08 (0,1)=6.01; (1,0)=3.86 (1,1)=4.85; (2,0)=2.73 (2,1)=3.63. Treatment effect ~+0.9 months in every stratum - direction confirmed and roughly homogeneous across feature_001.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable linear model, feature_015, feature_014, feature_001, feature_027, feature_019, feature_025, feature_006, feature_023, feature_022, and feature_005 retain independent effects on pfs_months in the directions identified marginally; many other features' marginal associations attenuate to non-significance.
feature_027's adjusted treatment effect (with all confounders held constant) remains close to its unadjusted +0.94 months, consistent with feature_027 being independently informative.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ const + f001_1 + f001_2 + all 15 binaries + 17 continuous features (statsmodels)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients (p<0.001 unless noted): feature_015 +0.176, feature_014 -1.362, feature_001(level1) -1.196, feature_001(level2) -2.329, feature_006 -0.287, feature_023 -0.354, feature_022 -0.357, feature_027 +0.943, feature_019 +0.469, feature_025 -0.076, feature_005 -0.005, feature_021 -0.0004. All other features attenuate to non-significant. R^2 = 0.86, adj R^2 = 0.86.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027 + all confounders
analyses.result_summary: feature_027 adjusted coef = +0.9429 (SE 0.010, t = 97.9, p~0). Effectively unchanged from the unadjusted +0.972, consistent with random treatment assignment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_027 is heterogeneous across patients: it is essentially abolished in subgroups defined by feature_006=1, feature_023=1, or feature_022=1 (resistance markers).
The treatment effect of feature_027 is enhanced in patients with low values of feature_005 (treatment-by-feature_005 interaction is negative).
The treatment effect of feature_027 is approximately constant across continuous-feature tertiles other than feature_005 (no other strong continuous modifiers).

analyses.code: Within feature_006=0: t-test of feature_027 effect; Within feature_006=1: t-test; same for feature_023 and feature_022; plus formal OLS interaction tests.
analyses.result_summary: feature_027 effect by feature_006: +1.480 (n=32343, p=4e-274) when feature_006=0 vs +0.026 (n=17657, p=0.52) when feature_006=1. feature_023: +1.656 (p~0) vs +0.027 (p=0.46). feature_022: +1.027 (p~0) vs -0.156 (p=0.13). OLS interaction coefficients (with controls): t*feature_006 = -1.46 (p~0), t*feature_023 = -1.63 (p~0), t*feature_022 = -0.99 (p=5e-104). Each marker individually nullifies the treatment effect.

analyses.code: Tertile cuts on feature_005, t-test of feature_027 effect within each tertile; OLS PFS ~ feature_027*feature_005 + controls
analyses.result_summary: feature_027 effect by feature_005 tertile: low [<2.65] +1.784 months (p=9e-185), mid [2.65-7.39] +1.104 (p=5e-93), high [>7.39] +0.007 (p=0.87). OLS interaction t*feature_005 = -0.0258 (p~0). Strong negative interaction confirmed.

analyses.code: Tertile cuts on each remaining continuous feature, t-test of feature_027 effect within each tertile; OLS PFS ~ feature_027*X + controls
analyses.result_summary: feature_015 tertiles: +1.01/+0.93/+0.94 (interaction OLS p=0.25); feature_019 tertiles: +1.02/+1.00/+0.89; feature_025: +0.96/+0.90/+1.06; feature_021: +0.95/+1.01/+0.96; feature_032/017/003/030/008/004/013/026/029/007/033/009 all show treatment differences within ~+/-0.1 of each other across tertiles. None of the formal interaction coefficients reaches a magnitude comparable to t*feature_005 or the binary mutation markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 (jointly negative on all three resistance markers) have a substantially larger feature_027 treatment effect than patients with at least one of those markers positive.
Within the jointly mutation-negative subgroup, the feature_027 treatment effect remains heterogeneous by feature_005, with low-feature_005 patients gaining the largest benefit.
Within the jointly mutation-positive subgroup (any of feature_006, feature_023, feature_022 = 1), the feature_027 treatment effect is null.

analyses.code: mut_neg = (f006==0)&(f023==0)&(f022==0); t-test of feature_027 effect within mut_neg and within ~mut_neg
analyses.result_summary: mut_neg (n=17372, 34.7% of cohort): mean PFS off=4.117, on=6.875, diff=+2.758 months (p~0). mut_pos (n=32628): off=4.117, on=4.147, diff=+0.030 months (p=0.29 - null). Treatment benefit is entirely localized to triple-negative patients.

analyses.code: Within mut_neg, tertile cuts on feature_005 and t-tests; also OLS in mut_neg subgroup with t*feature_005
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect within mut_neg by feature_005 tertile: low (<2.65) +5.06 (n=5734, p~0), mid (2.65-7.39) +2.99 (n=5876, p=3e-161), high (>7.39) -0.013 (n=5762, p=0.85). OLS within mut_neg: feature_027 main effect = +3.32, t*feature_005 interaction = -0.0645 per unit (p~0). Treatment effect is concentrated below feature_005 ~5.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a sharp threshold around feature_005 = 5 within the mut_neg subgroup: below 5 the treatment effect is approximately +5 months, and above 5 the treatment effect is essentially zero (rather than a smooth linear interaction).

feature_011 = 1 does not independently enhance the feature_027 treatment effect beyond what is explained by the mut_neg + feature_005 < 5 subgroup definition (i.e., its earlier marginal modifier appearance was confounded).

analyses.code: Threshold scan: for th in [3.5,4.0,4.5,4.8,5.0,5.2,5.5,6.0,7.0], compare trt effect below vs above threshold within mut_neg; finer bins in [0,1,2,3,4,5,7,10,20,1000].
analyses.result_summary: Bin-level treatment effects within mut_neg: f005<=1 +4.95, (1,2] +5.10, (2,3] +5.02, (3,4] +5.08, (4,5] +4.83, (5,7] -0.06, (7,10] +0.09, (10,20] +0.05, (20,1000] -0.11. Step change at feature_005=5: at threshold=5 the below-5 group has trt effect +5.013 (n=9326, p~0) while above-5 group has +0.005 (n=8046, p=0.93). The transition is essentially a step function around feature_005=5.

analyses.code: Within mut_neg, t-test of feature_027 effect stratified by feature_011; within (mut_neg & feature_005<5), t-test stratified by feature_011.
analyses.result_summary: mut_neg & feature_011=0: trt diff = +2.749 (n=16443); mut_neg & feature_011=1: +2.906 (n=929). Within fully responsive subgroup (mut_neg & feature_005<5): feature_011=0 +5.00 vs feature_011=1 +5.24 - statistically distinguishable but practically negligible (~0.2 month). The earlier marginal +1.93 vs +0.94 difference came from feature_011=1 being enriched for triple-negative status, not a true effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete responsive subgroup is defined by feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<5; in this subgroup feature_027 prolongs PFS by approximately 5 months.
Outside this subgroup (any of feature_006/023/022 =1, OR feature_005>=5), the feature_027 treatment effect is null (point estimate within ~+/-0.05 months of zero, non-significant).
Within the responsive subgroup, no other feature meaningfully modifies the +5-month treatment effect (subgroup is sufficient).

analyses.code: resp = (f006==0)&(f023==0)&(f022==0)&(f005<5); t-test feature_027 effect within and outside resp
analyses.result_summary: Responsive subgroup n=9326 (18.65%): off n=7423 mean=4.088, on n=1903 mean=9.101, diff=+5.013 months (p~0). Non-responsive n=40674 (81.35%): off mean=4.122, on mean=4.146, diff=+0.024 months (p=0.38). Almost the entire pooled main effect (+0.97) is attributable to this subgroup: 0.1865 * 5.01 + 0.8135 * 0 ~ +0.93.

analyses.code: Within responsive subgroup: stratified treatment-effect estimates by every other feature (binary subsets and continuous tertiles); formal OLS interaction tests t*X with base controls.
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect within responsive subgroup is uniformly between +4.85 and +5.28 across all binary strata (feature_001 levels: +4.87/+5.09/+4.81; feature_014: +5.03/+5.01; feature_011: +5.00/+5.24; feature_028, _002, _016, _024, _010, _012, _018, _020, _031 all give differences within ~0.2 months) and across all continuous tertiles (feature_015, _019, _025, _021, _032, _017, _003, _030, _008, _004, _013, _026, _029, _007, _033, _009 all between +4.85 and +5.18). Formal interactions: detectable but tiny coefficients (e.g., t*feature_015 = +0.0054 per unit, t*feature_014 = -0.028, t*feature_001 level 2 = -0.071) - these correspond to <0.3 months of variation across the whole feature range and are far smaller than the +5 month main effect. No further binary/continuous variable produces a clinically meaningful refinement.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 is randomly (or as-good-as-randomly) assigned: no other feature shows baseline imbalance with feature_027 at conventional significance.

feature_027 is the only binary variable in the dataset whose adjusted main effect on pfs_months is non-zero, supporting it as the unique treatment variable; the other binaries with adjusted effects (feature_014, feature_006, feature_023, feature_022) are biomarkers/prognostic factors rather than treatments.

analyses.code: For each non-outcome feature, test association with feature_027 (chi-square for categorical, t-test for continuous).
analyses.result_summary: Smallest p-values: feature_001 p=0.124, feature_019 p=0.138, feature_030 p=0.149, feature_015 p=0.159, feature_018 p=0.185 - all > 0.10. No baseline imbalance detected across 32 covariates, consistent with random treatment assignment.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ binary + 11 controls (feature_015, feature_014, feature_006, feature_023, feature_022, feature_019, feature_025, feature_005, feature_021, f001 dummies) for each binary in turn.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main effects (excluding the four already-confounder binaries f014/f006/f023/f022 in the controls): feature_027 +0.943 (p~0); all other binaries (feature_031 +0.012, feature_011 +0.029, feature_028 -0.010, feature_002 +0.023, feature_024 -0.013, feature_010 +0.004, feature_012 -0.000, feature_018 -0.002, feature_020 +0.025, feature_016 -0.010) have adjusted |coef| < 0.03 with p > 0.10. feature_027 is the unique binary with a real independent effect, supporting its role as the treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL responsive-subgroup hypothesis: among patients with feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<5, treatment feature_027 prolongs progression-free survival (pfs_months) by approximately +5.01 months on average; in all other patients the treatment effect is null. Each of feature_006=1, feature_023=1, feature_022=1, and feature_005>=5 individually appears sufficient to suppress the treatment benefit.

analyses.code: Combined: stratified effect estimates within each of the four predicates AND in each of the four single-predicate violations to verify that each predicate is necessary for benefit.
analyses.result_summary: Each predicate alone is sufficient to abolish benefit: with f006=1 alone (others 0): trt diff +0.083 (n=9458, p=0.12). With f023=1 alone: +0.061 (n=13490, p=0.17). With f022=1 alone: -0.204 (n=1481, p=0.12). With f005>=5 alone (others 0): trt diff in mut_neg with f005>=5 = -0.01 (n=5762, p=0.85). All four predicates jointly satisfied (responsive subgroup): trt diff +5.01 (n=9326, p~0). Direction and magnitude confirmed; subgroup definition complete.
